reasoning: North Korea has not conducted a nuclear test since September 2017. While intelligence agencies have repeatedly released assessments since 2022 (including recently in March 2025) stating that North Korea has completed preparations at the Punggye-ri site and is ready to test 'on short notice,' these warnings have thus far described a state of physical readiness rather than an imminent political decision to act. Similar warnings peaked around the US election in late 2024, yet no test occurred. Given the short 2-month forecasting window (May 1 to July 1, 2025) and the absence of definitive, near-term triggers (such as new observable deployment activities that depart from the baseline of the past three years), the probability of a test occurring in this specific timeframe is exceptionally low. The historical base rate over the last eight years is 0%, and factoring in the possibility of sudden provocation yields a final estimate at the lowest bound of 5%.
